quote_of_day.text: >>23088 brootal meng i am working on my physique so i could model some day. I know i might be bit old for this. But i dont wanna be 30 and look back at my 25 year self maybe i should have worked on my physique
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of discontent with moderators moved over the board, bringing scattered showers of criticism towards fagyush's moderation style. Anons expressed frustration with post deletions and bans, sharing their grievances in a lengthy discussion.

A cold snap of loneliness and isolation settled over parts of the board, as one anon sought advice on coping with lost youth and social isolation.

The mood was sombre, with a few respondents offering words of comfort. Meanwhile, a brief but intense downpour of despair hit the board, as an anon shared a phone number and another expressed feelings of inadequacy.

The responses ranged from abusive to briefly supportive, before dissipating into the usual fog of indifference.

articles.title: Anons share grievances against fagyush's moderation on defunct imageboard
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread to discuss their past issues with fagyush, the former administrator of a now-defunct imageboard, who was accused of randomly nuking posts and threads, and handing out harsh bans.

Anons shared their personal experiences of being banned from the imageboard, often for reasons they felt were unjust or petty. One anon claimed to have been banned for discussing a controversial topic, criticizing the BJP, and making a joke about someone's name.

Another anon reported being banned for a month for "muslim uppercaste shitposting.

articles.title: Coping with lost youth and social isolation
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking fellow posters how they cope with lost youth, lost teenage years, and lack of human interaction during those years, and how it affects their mental health.

One anon replied that they have severe social anxiety, which has been present throughout their life, making school and social interactions difficult. They enjoyed classes more than recess because it meant avoiding sitting alone and being teased.

Another anon suggested that 25 is a cutoff point after which one must be careful with life choices, including diet. They claimed to have cut off carbs and processed sugar, leading to better health.

A respondent expressed frustration with their life situation, stating that losing one's youth and 20s can be particularly difficult. They described their existence as a kind of purgatory, neither hell nor heaven.

One poster advised focusing on well-being and not getting too caught up in past regrets. Another simply stated they don't think about it much, being too busy with work.

A different anon mentioned racing against time and trying to take action rather than dwell on the past. They expressed interest in pursuing relationships with women who may have missed out on social experiences.

However, another poster warned that this approach might lead to disappointment. Some anons shared their own experiences, such as working on their physique to improve their life prospects.

The thread also touched on the topic of being a NEET and the challenges of social isolation. One poster mentioned their height and how it has affected their life, while another discussed the potential existence of unmarried, socially inexperienced women in their 30s.

articles.title: Anon Bemoans Mumbai's Fate
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion with a brief statement: "what a dehat,over for mumbairoaches".

One anon responded with a claim about Mumbai's history, stating that it "was chamar city which was under mullah rule few decades ago" and that "it was aryan officers who freed the chamars frrom mullah rule." Another anon expressed frustration about unemployment, saying "too much unemployment yaar people are frustrated if you will touch their nerves they let it out somewhere".

The conversation took a turn with an anon remarking, "No wonder incels survived all these years, we never engage in anything. Its a chad kill chad world".

This drew various responses, including an insult about someone's mother and a remark about "pajeet" having a "gobar dimag".

An anon questioned why Mumbai's infrastructure is not better, asking "how and why doesn't mumbai have better infra like delhi metro ?" This was met with a reply that Delhi is populated by "aryan UC jeets" while Mumbai is full of "chamars".

The discussion was marked by several derogatory remarks and insults, including threats of violence and personal attacks.

Anon predictions of doom for Mumbai were also voiced, with one stating "it is over for slumbai. that city is going down the drain" and another claiming "Everyday a dozen of pajeets die in mumbai local".

The tone of the conversation remained aggressive throughout, with little attempt at constructive discussion.

articles.title: Anon posts phone number on /b/
articles.body:
Anon posted a phone number, 011-23361880, in a thread on Indiachan's /b/ board.

Another anon responded with a comment about privileged individuals having an easier life, stating "soylennial pajeets had it good yaar. they could get any stupid degree and have a jaab." A respondent expressed frustration with their own life, saying "I work 10-12 hours a day and still get fired and what not." One anon advised being dishonest to succeed in India, saying "honesty is the worst policy in a country like pajeetia. never follow rules."

articles.title: Anon expresses despair, others respond with mix of abuse and fleeting concern
articles.body: Anon posted about feeling like a burden and ruining everything. One respondent shared a similar struggle with daily failures. Another anon suggested listening to music to feel better and not killing oneself. Some anons hurled abuse and threats at others. One poster claimed evolution of the soul is real and one can become perfect. Another suggested watching old movies or masturbating to feel better.
